name: Venison Jerky, from Andrea Cassoni
recipeInstructions:
1. Slice the meat when it is lightly frozen. The cuts should be long, thin and with the grain. Cut across the grain if you want more tender, but more brittle jerky.
2. Trim off all of the fat.
3. Marinate strips in a glass container overnight. You may substitute 2 cups of red wine for the corn whiskey and water.
4. Pat dry and arrange pieces side by side on an oven roasting rack, with no overlap.
5. Cook at minimum heat (150°F) for 6 hours. Leave oven door slightly open to allow moisture to escape.
6. Meat should be dark, dry, and store jerky in a cool, airtight container.
